rawIntent:
[Feature: Build the leave management module — Phase 1: Create LeaveRequest domain model and repository]

Create approximately 3 files in src/modules/leave: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts defining the canonical LeaveRequest entity and DTO/types used by persistence, src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts defining repository interfaces and PostgreSQL-backed operations including createLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, and rejectLeaveRequest, and tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts covering repository behavior. Keep the model and repository in the same phase so repository methods reference the actual LeaveRequest definitions.

Phase architecture notes:
Establishes persistence contracts and storage implementation for LeaveRequest.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):
{"interfaces":["File: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts\nexport type LeaveRequestStatus = \"PENDING\" | \"APPROVED\" | \"REJECTED\";\nexport type LeaveType = \"ANNUAL\" | \"SICK\" | \"EMERGENCY\";\nexport interface LeaveRequest { id: string; employeeId: string; leaveType: LeaveType; startDate: Date; endDate: Date; status: LeaveRequestStatus; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; }\nexport interface CreateLeaveRequestInput { id: string; employeeId: string; leaveType: LeaveType; startDate: Date; endDate: Date; }\nexport interface AuditRecord { entityId: string; entityType: \"LEAVE_REQUEST\"; action: \"CREATE\" | \"APPROVE\" | \"REJECT\"; performedBy: string; createdAt: Date; }","File: src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts\nimport { AuditRecord, CreateLeaveRequestInput, LeaveRequest } from \"./leave.model\";\nexport interface LeaveRequestRepository { createLeaveRequest(input: CreateLeaveRequestInput, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest>; findById(id: string): Promise<LeaveRequest | null>; approveLeaveRequest(id: string, approvedBy: string, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest>; rejectLeaveRequest(id: string, rejectedBy: string, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest>; findByEmployeeId(employeeId: string): Promise<LeaveRequest[]>; }\nexport class PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository implements LeaveRequestRepository { constructor() {} createLeaveRequest(input: CreateLeaveRequestInput, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest> { throw new Error(\"Not implemented\"); } findById(id: string): Promise<LeaveRequest | null> { throw new Error(\"Not implemented\"); } approveLeaveRequest(id: string, approvedBy: string, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest> { throw new Error(\"Not implemented\"); } rejectLeaveRequest(id: string, rejectedBy: string, auditRecord: AuditRecord): Promise<LeaveRequest> { throw new Error(\"Not implemented\"); } findByEmployeeId(employeeId: string): Promise<LeaveRequest[]> { throw new Error(\"Not implemented\"); } }","File: tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts\nimport { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\";\nimport { PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository } from \"../../../../src/modules/leave/leave.repository\";\ndescribe(\"PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository\", () => { it(\"defines repository behavior contracts\", () => { expect(PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository).toBeDefined(); }); });"],"importStatements":["import { AuditRecord, CreateLeaveRequestInput, LeaveRequest } from \"./leave.model\"","import { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\"","import { PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository } from \"../../../../src/modules/leave/leave.repository\""],"successCriteria":["src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveRequest, LeaveRequestStatus, LeaveType, CreateLeaveRequestInput, and AuditRecord","src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository plus the concrete PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository implementation","LeaveRequestRepository defines createLeaveRequest, findById, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest, and findByEmployeeId with the exact TypeScript signatures specified in this phase","All state-changing repository methods (createLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest) require an AuditRecord parameter to satisfy audit-record requirements","tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts exists and executes with Vitest, validating the repository contract exports and behavior scaffolding"]}

## Deferred to later phases
The following concerns are intentionally OUT OF SCOPE for this phase and will be addressed in subsequent phases:
- Phase 2 — Create LeaveBalance domain model and repository: Create approximately 3 files in src/modules/balance: src/modules/balance/leave-balance.model.ts defi
- Phase 3 — Create AuditRecord domain model and repository: Create approximately 3 files in src/modules/audit: src/modules/audit/audit-record.model.ts defining 
- Phase 4 — Implement leave submission workflow service: Create approximately 3 files centered on submission logic. Create src/modules/leave/leave.service.ts
- Phase 5 — Implement approval and rejection workflow service methods: Modify or extend approximately 2-3 files to add approveLeaveRequest and rejectLeaveRequest behavior 
- Phase 6 — Add employee leave API endpoints: Create approximately 3 files for Fastify exposure of employee actions. Create src/modules/leave/leav
- Phase 7 — Add manager approval and balance API endpoints with RBAC: Extend approximately 3-4 files to expose manager approval and rejection endpoints and leave balance 
- Phase 8 — Create Notification domain model and repository: Create approximately 3 files in src/modules/notification: src/modules/notification/notification.mode
- Phase 9 — Integrate notifications and balance mutations into workflow: Modify approximately 4-5 files to generate Notification records for submission, approval, and reject
- Phase 10 — Add end-to-end leave management test coverage: Create approximately 4-5 Vitest test files covering complete workflows. This phase depends on src/mo

successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestStatus, LeaveType, LeaveRequest, CreateLeaveRequestInput, and AuditRecord with the exact TypeScript definitions specified for this phase.

src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository with createLeaveRequest, findById, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest, and findByEmployeeId using the exact signatures defined in the phase architecture.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exports PostgreSQLLeaveRequestRepository implementing LeaveRequestRepository and containing the specified method stubs.
All state-changing repository methods (createLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest) require an AuditRecord parameter.
tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts exists, uses Vitest, and validates repository export and behavior scaffolding as specified.

constraints: Use the repository pattern: all database access must be represented through repository interfaces.
All state-changing operations must require and support audit records; createLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, and rejectLeaveRequest must accept an AuditRecord parameter.
Repository contracts and implementation must use the LeaveRequest model definitions from src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts.
Keep scope limited to LeaveRequest model and repository persistence contracts/scaffolding in the leave module.
Tests must be written using Vitest.
No unhandled promise rejections; async repository methods must be designed for handled error paths.
Do not implement API endpoints, workflow services, balance mutations, notifications, or cross-module business logic in this phase.
outOfScope: Dedicated audit module or audit repository implementation.
Leave workflow service implementation (submission, approval, rejection business logic).
Fastify API endpoints and RBAC endpoint enforcement.
Notification domain model, repository, and notification generation.
Balance deductions and balance repository integration.
Employee validation, manager approval rules, and policy evaluation logic.

Any files outside src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts, src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts, and tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts except minimal supporting imports/types required by those files.
